active_paradigms.idea: scipy.optimize.minimize with trust-constr for joint position-radius optimization
active_paradigms.description: Optimize all 78 variables (x, y, r for each of 26 circles) jointly using trust-constr method which handles many nonlinear constraints efficiently. Define objective as negative sum of radii. Constraints: (1) 325 pairwise non-overlap constraints: ||c_i - c_j|| >= r_i + r_j, (2) 104 boundary constraints: x-r>=0, x+r<=1, y-r>=0, y+r<=1. Bounds: 0<=x,y<=1, r>=0. Use current layout as initial guess. Trust-constr is specifically designed for problems with many constraints and uses a trust-region approach that handles nonlinear constraints better than SLSQP.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize sum of radii by optimizing both positions and radii simultaneously
active_paradigms.cautions: With 429 constraints, ensure constraint Jacobians are computed efficiently. Set maxiter=1000, gtol=1e-6. May need multiple restarts from different initial layouts. Monitor for constraint violations in final output.

active_paradigms.description: Use position-only formulation with 52 variables (x, y for each circle). Radii are computed implicitly as r_i = min(wall_distance, min_{j≠i}||c_i - c_j||), guaranteeing valid solutions. Define objective function that computes radii from positions and returns negative sum. Bounds: [(0,1)]×52. Use strategy='best1bin', maxiter=500, popsize=10, tol=1e-7, mutation=(0.5,1), recombination=0.7. This global optimizer can escape local optima and find fundamentally better position configurations than the fixed layout.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Find globally optimal positions that maximize sum of radii through population-based search
active_paradigms.cautions: Objective is non-smooth (piecewise linear) due to min operations. Use seed=42 for reproducibility. Set polish=True for local refinement. Runtime may be 60-120 seconds for 500 iterations with population ~520.

active_paradigms.idea: scipy.optimize.basinhopping for global position optimization with local refinement
active_paradigms.description: Combine global exploration with local minimization using basinhopping. Optimize 52 position variables with implicit radii computation. Use L-BFGS-B as local minimizer with bounds [(0,1)]×52. Parameters: niter=200, T=1.0 (temperature for accepting worse solutions), stepsize=0.1. The algorithm performs random perturbations followed by local minimization, allowing escape from local minima. Use take_step callback with adaptive step size. This hybrid approach can find better layouts than pure local optimization while being faster than differential_evolution.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Global search over position space with local refinement at each step
active_paradigms.cautions: Tune temperature T based on objective scale (sum_radii ~2-3, so T=0.5-1.0). Use seed for reproducibility. The non-smooth objective may cause L-BFGS-B warnings; consider Nelder-Mead as alternative local minimizer. Track best solution across all iterations.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with SLSQP for joint position-radius optimization
tried_paradigms.description: Optimize all 78 variables (52 position coordinates + 26 radii) simultaneously using SLSQP. Define inequality constraints: boundary constraints (x_i - r_i >= 0, x_i + r_i <= 1) are linear; non-overlap constraints (||c_i - c_j|| >= r_i + r_j) are nonlinear. Pack variables as [x1,y1,...,x26,y26,r1,...,r26]. Use current layout as initial guess with multiple random restarts. The optimizer will adjust both positions AND radii to maximize sum.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii through joint optimization of all positions and sizes
tried_paradigms.cautions: Non-overlap constraints are non-convex; use 10-20 random restarts. Set ftol=1e-8 for precision. The 325 non-overlap constraints require careful implementation. Provide analytic Jacobians if possible. May need maxiter=1000+ for convergence.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.differential_evolution with constraint penalty function
tried_paradigms.description: Formulate as bound-constrained global optimization. Variables: positions in [0,1], radii in [0,0.5]. Objective: -sum(radii) + penalty_weight * sum(max(0, violation)^2) for all constraints. Use strategy='best1bin', popsize=15, mutation=(0.5,1), recombination=0.7. Differential evolution explores globally and escapes local minima that trap SLSQP. Run with workers=-1 for parallelization.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii through global stochastic search across the entire solution space
tried_paradigms.cautions: Penalty weight critical - start with 1000, increase if constraints violated. 78 dimensions is high; consider reducing by exploiting symmetry (place circles in symmetric pairs). Set maxiter=2000+ and tol=1e-7. Computationally expensive but finds better global solutions.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.spatial.Voronoi with Lloyd relaxation for geometric position optimization
tried_paradigms.description: Exploit the geometric relationship between circle packing and Voronoi diagrams. Start with initial positions, compute Voronoi tessellation using scipy.spatial.Voronoi. For each cell, compute the maximum inscribed circle radius (distance to nearest Voronoi edge or boundary). Apply Lloyd relaxation: move each center toward its cell centroid weighted by radius potential. Iterate until convergence. Finally, use local radius optimization on the converged positions.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: positions that maximize available space for each circle through geometric structure
tried_paradigms.cautions: Standard Voronoi ignores radii; for true optimality need power diagram (weighted Voronoi). Handle boundary cells (extend to infinity) by clipping to unit square. Lloyd may converge to local optima - try multiple initializations. Combine with final SLSQP refinement on radii only for best results.
